author: lia
content: Hey @marcus and @dana. I know we're being asked to 'drop everything' for the patches while also hitting the upsell deadline. I'm worried about the collision in the subsystem. Can we sync for 5 mins on the reality of doing both so I can take a clear trade-off request to leadership?

author: marcus
content: @lia Real talk: the subsystem is too unstable to support the upsell logic right now. If we force D2 before D1 is finished, we're just building a faster way to crash. I can give you the technical 'why' for leadership, but we need to pick a sequence, not a parallel track.

author: dana
content: @marcus @lia 100% with Marcus. D2 touches the same code paths as the flaky integration. If I start the upsell work now, I'm just adding variables to his debugging nightmare. I'm pivoting D2 to 'backlog' until we have a green light on the patch stability, or we're going to burn the house down trying to renovate it.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana Thanks for the clarity. 'Building a faster way to crash' is the exact phrasing I'll use. I'm drafting a formal priority reset for the Director now. I'll frame it as: we secure the foundation (D1) first to ensure the upsell (D2) actually stays online. Stay focused on the patches; I'll handle the heat from above.

author: marcus
content: @lia Appreciate you taking point on that. If they push back, tell them D1 is 80% of the reason for our current high latency. I'm heads down on the guardrails now so we can get to a 'safe' state ASAP.

author: dana
content: @lia Thanks. If they ask for a timeline, tell them the upsell (D2) dev time actually shrinks if the subsystem is stable first. I'm standing by to peer review Marcus's patches so we can clear D1 faster.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana I've sent the 'Sequence over Parallel' proposal to the Director. I'm making it clear that starting D2 now actively jeopardizes D1. I'll let you know the second I have a formal sign-off on the pivot.

author: lia
content: I'm worried the Director will try to 'split the difference.' I need to hold the line that D1 is the prerequisite for D2's success, not just a competing task.

author: marcus
content: @lia Glad to hear. @dana, I've just pushed the first set of guardrails to the repo. If you can tear those apart in review now, we might actually get ahead of the 'bleeding' by EOD.

author: dana
content: @marcus On it. Pulling up the PR now. I'll prioritize these reviews so we can get D1 to 'done' and give @lia the data she needs to show leadership we're moving fast on the right things.

author: lia
content: Agreed. I'm framing D1 as the 'stability tax' we have to pay to make D2 viable. If we ship D2 on a broken foundation, the churn will outweigh the upsell revenue. I'm not giving them an 'and' option.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana Great progress on the PRs. I'm meeting with the Director in 30 mins. I'm bringing your point, Dana, that D2 dev time actually *shrinks* once D1 is solid. I will get us that formal 'D1 first' mandate.

author: marcus
content: @lia Good luck with the Director. If they need a 'worst-case' scenario: without D1's guardrails, the upsell load will likely trigger a cascading failure in the DB. We're close on the first set of fixes.

author: dana
content: @lia Tell the Director that by focusing on D1, we've already cleared the first batch of critical fixes in record time. Subsystem stability is trending up. @marcus, first PR looks solid, I've left two minor comments on the retry logic, then it's good to merge.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana I'm in the lobby now. I'm presenting the 'Stability First' roadmap as the only way to protect the upsell revenue. I will not leave the room without a clear 'D1 is Priority 1' confirmation in writing. Keep that momentum on the PRs—it's my strongest leverage.

author: lia
content: I'm refusing to provide a 'blended' timeline. It's D1 then D2, or we fail both. I'm prepared for the Director to be unhappy, but I'm framing it as protecting the quarter's bottom line.

author: marcus
content: @dana Fixed the retry logic and merged. Pushing the final guardrail set now. @lia Give 'em hell. We're proving that focus actually gets code shipped faster.

author: dana
content: @marcus Final guardrail PR pulled up. I'm doing a deep dive now. @lia We're showing them that one thing done is better than two things broken. Good luck.
